content: For REMS-required medications: (1) Verify prescriber is certified in the REMS program before dispensing; (2) Confirm patient enrollment and completion of required counseling; (3) Ensure baseline and ongoing monitoring requirements are met (e.g., ANC for clozapine, pregnancy tests for isotretinoin); (4) Document all REMS-related activities in medical record; (5) Dispense only authorized quantities between monitoring intervals; (6) Report adverse events to REMS program; (7) Reauthorize prescriptions per program requirements. Pharmacies must be certified to dispense REMS drugs. Failure to comply with ETASU can result in FDA enforcement actions and loss of prescribing/dispensing privileges.
